management_notes:
Institutional investors own approximately 59% of Torex Gold, the public approximately 40%, and insiders approximately 0.43% (~C$27 million at current prices, ~413,000 shares on a 96.2M share base). While modest as a percentage, C$27M is meaningful in absolute terms. CEO Jody Kuzenko (formerly VP Operations) led the successful delivery of the Media Luna Underground project — approximately USD $900 million of construction in Mexico's Guerrero State over approximately three years — on time and on budget. Media Luna achieved commercial production on May 1, 2025, enabling the company to generate record cash flows in the back half of 2025.

In 2025, management demonstrated disciplined capital allocation: all project debt (USD $30 million remaining balance) was fully repaid in January 2026; the company initiated an inaugural quarterly dividend of USD $0.15/share (December 2025); more than 825,000 shares were repurchased via NCIB; and a record USD $77 million exploration budget was announced for 2026. No material insider selling has been reported. The company engaged the Canadian investor community at PDAC 2026 and maintains active sell-side coverage. Per SEDI, no significant open-market disposals by named executives were found in recent press coverage.

The management score of 3/5 reflects a combination of modest but present insider equity stake, exceptional operational execution, and shareholder-friendly capital return track record. A higher score would require more significant personal equity ownership at the individual executive level (1-3%+). Governance is standard TSX mid-tier with no identified red flags.
geology_notes:
Year-end 2025 Proven & Probable Reserves at the Morelos Complex: 4,839 koz AuEq at an average grade of 3.80 g/t AuEq, per the March 24, 2026 NI 43-101 reserve statement (Qualified Persons: Rochelle Collins P.Geo., Principal Mineral Resource Geologist; Johannes Bekkers P.Eng., both Torex employees as defined by NI 43-101). Net of depletion, reserves decreased 5% from year-end 2024 (5,096 koz AuEq at 3.92 g/t), reflecting approximately 376,000 oz AuEq mined in 2025 and positive additions of 207 koz (+4%) from drilling success at ELG Underground and Media Luna infill drilling. Reserve metal price assumptions: US$1,650/oz Au, US$21.00/oz Ag, US$3.85/lb Cu — highly conservative relative to April 2026 spot prices (Au ~$3,200/oz, Ag ~$32/oz, Cu ~$4.20/lb). The reserve base is robust to any plausible near-term commodity price softness.

Total Measured & Indicated Resources at Morelos: 7,262 koz AuEq. Los Reyes Project (Sinaloa, Mexico): 2,047 koz AuEq Indicated + 765 koz AuEq Inferred. Inaugural Inferred Resource at Media Luna West: 506 koz AuEq at 5.11 g/t (exceptional high-grade target for a potential underground extension). Total Inferred resource base grew 30% to 2.9 Moz AuEq company-wide. At 2025 payable production of 376,364 AuEq oz, the Morelos P&P reserves alone represent approximately 13 years of reserve mine life. The M&I resource base of 7.3 Moz provides significant conversion potential over that mine life.

The 3.80 g/t AuEq reserve grade is high for a combined open-pit/underground producer in Mexico and reflects the contribution of the high-grade Media Luna underground deposit. With a Feasibility Study-backed reserve base confirmed by live production (Media Luna commercial production May 2025), a clear resource conversion pipeline, and a conservative price deck, this is a 5/5 geology score. No study-tier discount applies — the mine is in production.
capital_notes:
Per Q4 and full-year 2025 results (released February 2026): common shares outstanding as of November 11, 2025 — 96,176,134 (public float: 81,334,308). Cash and cash equivalents at December 31, 2025: USD $119.5 million. Total available liquidity: USD $426.3 million (USD $119.5M cash + USD $306.8M undrawn on the USD $350M credit facility). Remaining debt at December 31, 2025: USD $30 million — fully repaid in January 2026. As of early 2026, Torex is effectively debt-free. No significant warrant or option overhang was identified in investor relations materials.

2025 annual financial performance: revenue USD $1.306 billion; adjusted EBITDA USD $730.3 million; net income USD $403.4 million; record Q4 2025 free cash flow USD $166 million. Full-year 2025 AISC: USD $1,783/oz AuEq (above the guided range, partly due to the positive mechanical linkage between higher gold prices and royalty costs; expected to trend toward $1,600/oz as Media Luna reaches steady-state operations). 2026 production guidance: 420,000-470,000 oz AuEq (vs. 376,364 oz in 2025). 2026 FCF generation at current gold prices is expected to be the highest in company history.

With 96.2 million shares outstanding (low for a mid-tier producer generating USD $1.3B+ in annual revenue), a debt-free balance sheet, USD $426M available liquidity, and USD $400M+ annual free cash flow capacity at current prices, Torex's capital structure is exceptional. The low share count ensures per-share metrics improve materially as production grows toward 420-470k AuEq oz. Score 5/5.
catalyst_notes:
Near-term catalyst stack (next 12 months): (1) Q1 2026 production and financial results — scheduled for May 6, 2026 (Q1 2026 production has already been reported; financial results will quantify FCF generation at $3,200+ gold prices on 420-470k AuEq oz guidance for the full year); (2) Los Reyes Preliminary Economic Assessment — expected by mid-2026, evaluating open-pit and underground development scenarios for the 2,047 koz AuEq Indicated + 765 koz AuEq Inferred project in Sinaloa, Mexico; (3) Los Reyes Pre-Feasibility Study — initiation planned for H2 2026, with full PFS targeted for approximately mid-2027. The $18 million allocated to Los Reyes in 2026 includes a 20,000-metre drill program and the PEA work.

Exploration-driven catalysts: USD $77 million 2026 exploration budget (largest in company history), including: Media Luna West drilling targeting expansion of the 506 koz AuEq Inferred @ 5.11 g/t inaugural resource; continued ELG Underground reserve delineation (two years of mine life added from 2025 infill drilling); initial drilling at Nevada properties (Gryphon and Medicine Springs) and Chihuahua early-stage assets (Batopilas and Guigui). Media Luna West results — expected H2 2026 — could be a material resource update if the high-grade (5.11 g/t) zone extends at depth.

The Los Reyes PEA by mid-2026 is the primary binary catalyst for the next six months, with the potential to define a development pathway for what could be Torex's third mine. Score 4/5 (5/5 would require an imminent FS release or drill results at the flagship Morelos Complex due within 30-60 days; the PEA is the nearest scheduled event at approximately 2-3 months).
acquisition_notes:
P/NAV analysis using Feasibility Study-level reserve basis — no study-tier discount applied (mine is in production). At US$3,000/oz gold (conservative relative to spot) and 2025 AISC of $1,783/oz AuEq: operating margin $1,217/oz. P&P Reserve NPV5% (4,839 koz over ~13-year LOM, LOM factor ~0.65): approximately USD $3.83 billion. M&I Resources at 50% probability weighting and 55% NPV factor: approximately USD $1.99 billion. Los Reyes Indicated at 25% probability weighting and 40% NPV factor: approximately USD $218 million. Total enterprise NAV: approximately USD $6.04 billion. Equity NAV (+ USD $119.5M cash, USD $0 debt): approximately USD $6.16 billion = C$8.56 billion (at 0.72 USD/CAD) / 96,176,134 shares = approximately C$89/share. Current price: C$65.35. Adjusted P/NAV: approximately 0.73x.

EV/P&P reserve comparison: EV approximately USD $4.41 billion / 4,839 koz P&P = approximately USD $912/oz P&P AuEq. For FS-backed mid-tier producers in LATAM, historical acquisition multiples at $2,500 gold ranged from USD $500-800/oz P&P. At $3,200+ spot gold, premiums have expanded — but TXG's EV/oz is already near the upper end of typical acquisition ranges for Mexico-based producers. Torex is not deeply discounted on EV/oz relative to recent deals (Kinross, Gold Fields, and Newmont LATAM asset transactions in 2024-2025).

The P/NAV of 0.73x at US$3,000 gold falls in the 3/5 band (0.5-1.0x). TXG's reserve quality is FS-level — the highest tier — which is stronger than the PEA/PFS criteria that formally maps to this band; however, the discount to NAV is not yet deep enough for a 4/5 score. Mexico jurisdiction risk (Guerrero State security environment, ongoing fiscal regime uncertainty post-2024 federal election) and AISC trending above $1,700/oz are the valuation headwinds. Score 3/5.
analyst_summary:
Torex Gold Resources (TSX: TXG) receives a composite score of 20/25 — BUY. The two standout factors are geology (5/5) and capital structure (5/5), both exceptional for a mid-tier gold producer. The Morelos Complex holds 4,839 koz AuEq in Proven & Probable Reserves at 3.80 g/t — backed by a Feasibility Study and confirmed by live production, with Media Luna declaring commercial production May 2025. The balance sheet is effectively debt-free as of January 2026 (USD $30M final repayment), with USD $119.5M cash, USD $426M available liquidity, 2025 net income of USD $403.4 million, and record Q4 FCF of USD $166 million. The company also holds a 7,262 koz AuEq M&I resource base at Morelos plus the 2,047 koz AuEq Los Reyes Indicated project and 506 koz AuEq Media Luna West Inferred at 5.11 g/t.

The weakest factors are management (3/5 — insiders hold a modest 0.43% equity stake directly, though the operational execution track record and capital allocation discipline are exceptional) and acquisition value (3/5 — P/NAV of approximately 0.73x at US$3,000 gold, with EV/oz at ~$912 near the historical ceiling for LATAM FS-stage producers). The full Feasibility Study reserve basis (no study-tier discount) and conservative reserve price deck ($1,650/oz gold vs. $3,200+ spot) provide strong downside protection to the reserve economics. Key risks are Mexico jurisdiction (Guerrero State security, fiscal regime changes) and an AISC of $1,783/oz in 2025 that is above the longer-term guidance range — expected to decline toward $1,600/oz as Media Luna reaches steady-state throughput.

The primary catalyst to watch is the Los Reyes PEA expected by mid-2026, which will define development economics for 2,047 koz AuEq Indicated + 765 koz Inferred in Sinaloa — a potential third mine in Torex's portfolio. The USD $77M 2026 exploration program (record) also drives catalysts through Media Luna West drilling (5.11 g/t Inferred zone) and ELG reserve delineation. Q1 2026 earnings on May 6, 2026 will be the first read on FCF generation at $3,200+ gold. At C$65.35 per share, TXG offers FS-backed reserve quality, a debt-free balance sheet, and a credible exploration pipeline at approximately 27% discount to estimated NAV.
